steps.title: Identify the structures around the blank
steps.content: The sentence opens with a participial phrase — "Powered with [energy collected by solar panels during the day]" — that modifies the main clause "the blinking LEDs keep lions away at night." The blank holds the noun phrase inside that participial phrase plus whatever punctuation separates it from the main clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rules
steps.content: Two rules apply: (1) an introductory participial phrase must be set off from the main clause by a single comma, and (2) the noun phrase "energy collected by solar panels during the day" is a tightly bound unit (noun + restrictive modifier) and cannot be broken up by internal commas.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — energy collected, by solar panels, during the day: wrongly inserts commas around "by solar panels," treating a restrictive modifier as parenthetical; it also lacks the required comma after the introductory participial phrase. ✗
  • Choice B — energy collected by solar panels during the day: keeps the noun phrase intact but omits the required comma between the introductory participial phrase and the main clause. ✗
  • Choice C — energy collected by solar panels during the day,: keeps the noun phrase intact and places the single required comma after the introductory phrase. ✓
  • Choice D — energy, collected by solar panels during the day,: wrongly inserts a comma between "energy" and its restrictive participial modifier "collected by solar panels during the day." ✗
That is the correct answer.
